response.justification: The candidate lacks the minimum C++ experience (approximately 6 months vs. 8 years required) and demonstrates no formal technical team leadership experience. While they show strong software craftsmanship, distributed systems knowledge, and architectural thinking, these cannot compensate for the missing core qualifications that are fundamental to this infrastructure-focused role.
strengths: Extensive low-latency distributed systems experience: built microservices infrastructure handling millions of concurrent requests at Dunnhumby with real-time data processing
Established engineering best practices across organizations: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code standards, and architectural patterns
Proven ability to conceive and drive complex, ambiguous technical projects: GISCollective platform founder, multiple open-source libraries, cross-domain expertise
concerns: Critical skills gap: negligible C++ experience (~6 months at National Instruments) versus 8-year minimum requirement; not listed in skills section
No formal technical team leadership track record: no team leads, manager roles, or evidence of mentoring engineers at scale; teaching workshops does not substitute
Career focused on product engineering (direct mail, GIS, web services) rather than infrastructure, compute platforms, or systems-level development
